item.title: Paradox Interactive Making LEGO City Skylines Spinoff, Ratings Board Leak Suggests
item.content:encoded:
Paradox Interactive is working on a Cities: Skylines LEGO spinoff, a newly-leaked ratings board listing for the game suggests.

LEGO Skylines has been rated by the ever-leaky Korean ratings board, which listed the game ahead of its formal announcement, as first spotted by Gematsu. IGN has contacted Paradox Interactive for more.

While details remain scarce, Cities: Skylines fans are digesting the idea of a LEGO-ified version of their favorite city-building simulation. Thematically, the idea of a LEGO city builder makes perfect sense — though fans naturally have concerns over the shape it'll arrive in, following the rocky reception to Cities: Skylines 2.

"How tf are they doing that when 2 is still in the shape it is in?" wrote one Cities: Skylines fan in a lengthy reddit thread discussing the leak. "Can't wait! See you three years after release when the game is somewhat playable!" quipped another.

Cities Skylines II Screenshots

While Paradox is listed as the game's publisher, it remains unclear who is actually developing the game — and whether any of Paradox's own studios are involved. The original Cities: Skylines and its sequel were created by Finnish studio Colossal Order, which parted ways with Paradox last year. Ongoing Cities: Skylines 2 development is now being handled by another Finnish outfit, Iceflake Studios, which is currently tasked with continuing improvements to the game — as well as readying its long-awaited console ports that were originally due in 2024.

"A LEGO City building game would knock my socks off, if they did it right," another fan wrote. "LEGO and the first Sim City games were my childhood, so this announcement has straight-up grabbed my hype right in the nostalgia," stated another.

While the original Cities: Skylines was a hit for Paradox, its troubled sequel launched in October 2023 to numerous complaints over its uneven performance and many missing features. IGN's Cities: Skylines 2 review returned a 6/10, and labelled it as "an ambitious sequel that might have bitten off more than it can chew."

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social

item.title: Take-Two CEO Pushes Back Against Suggestion Red Dead Online Was a Missed Opportunity
item.content:encoded:
Take-Two CEO Pushes Back Against Suggestion Red Dead Online Was a Missed Opportunity

Spurred on.

Strauss Zelnick, the boss of Rockstar Games parent company Take-Two, has pushed back at the suggestion Red Dead Online was a missed opportunity, insisting it “has been immensely successful and long lasting.”

Red Dead Online is the standalone multiplayer component of Rockstar’s 2018 masterpiece, Red Dead Redemption 2. While Rockstar initially released significant updates for the game, the last major content expansion for Red Dead Online was the Blood Money update released in July 2021. Since then, Rockstar has focused on a monthly event schedule. Last year’s Strange Tales of the West update came as a welcome surprise that sparked hope for more significant updates to come, but none have materialized.

And so, the consensus among its community is that Red Dead Online hasn't enjoyed quite the same amount of attention that GTA Online, the multiplayer component of 2013’s Grand Theft Auto V, has. When last month’s Rockstar leak, which came from a data theft by hackers, revealed that GTA Online eclipses Red Dead Online in terms of revenue and player numbers, fans weren’t surprised at all, with many pointing out that Red Dead Online hadn’t seen any content updates in some time, and that it suffers from a significant griefing problem.

Every IGN Rockstar Game Review Ever

Could Red Dead Online have ever matched GTA Online for Take-Two, and should it be considered a missed opportunity? In a recent interview with IGN ahead of its latest financial report, Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick pushed back on the suggestion, pointing to Red Dead Redemption 2’s incredible sales.

“So let me be clear,” he began, “there is literally nothing about Red Dead selling 85 million units that could signal a missed opportunity. And Red Dead Online has been immensely successful and long lasting.”

Zelnick went on to say that the feeling around Red Dead would be very different it it weren’t for GTA’s incredible success.

“I think if we didn't have Grand Theft Auto here at our company, then people would just talk about the fact that we have this massive franchise in Red Dead, which we do and of which we're very proud,” he said. “I actually personally think Red Dead is just amazing and I love engaging with it. And I think the reason it continues to sell is that it's just spectacular entertainment. It's beautiful and it feels very up to date despite the fact that it's not a new title.”

As part of Take-Two’s financial report, it confirmed Red Dead Redemption 2 had now sold over 85 million copies. That means it has officially overtaken Wii Sports to become the third best-selling video game of all time, behind only Minecraft and Grand Theft Auto 5 itself, which has now sold an incredible 230 million copies. So while GTA 5 has sold 145 million more copies than Red Dead Redemption 2, Rockstar’s Wild West adventure is one of gaming’s biggest ever hits.

Still, the focus on GTA Online over Red Dead Online at Rockstar will probably never sit well with Red Dead Online fans. According to that data leak, Red Dead Online has just shy of 1 million weekly players, which is a huge number for any game. It’s a huge, ongoing live service that just doesn’t get meaningful updates.

Indeed, Red Dead Redemption 2's ongoing success only makes Rockstar's decision not to release a 60fps patch for the modern console version more baffling. Fans have for some time now called on the developer to update the game on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X and S, and despite various rumors that such an upgrade is in the works, Red Dead Redemption 2 remains stuck at 30fps on current-gen consoles.

There is often talk of Red Dead Redemption 3, which feels like an inevitability. Late last year, Rockstar Games co-founder and former lead writer Dan Houser said Red Dead Redemption 3 “will probably happen.” And in 2023, Roger Clark, who played Arthur Morgan, the protagonist of Red Dead Redemption 2, said he was “certain” fans will see Red Dead Redemption 3 “one day,” but qualified that statement by saying he had no idea when that would be.

Even Hollywood actor Jack Black has had his say, revealing the character he’d love to play in Red Dead Redemption 3 should it ever happen.

For now, Rockstar’s attention is squarely on GTA 6, but maybe once it’s over the launch and whatever’s next for GTA Online is out the door, it will revisit its iconic Wild West franchise. And if it does, perhaps Rockstar will have a new version of Red Dead Online up its sleeve.

Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.

Recommends

item.title: James Bond Meets Khaby Lame in 007 First Light
item.content:encoded:
James Bond will meet social media influencer Khaby Lame in 007 First Light, developer IO Interactive has revealed.

It's a reveal that has left some Bond fans scratching their heads — firstly that Lame is in the game at all, but also secondly that his cameo has been revealed ahead of the game's general release — removing the element of surprise.

Lame is famous for his deadpan TikTok reaction videos, where he performs an exasperated gesture to mock things that seem obvious. Unsurprisingly, it's this same gesture that Bond fans will be able to see Lame replicating in 007 First Light, as His Majesty's agent asks for directions while standing in front of a signpost.

In a video clip posted to Instagram, players can see Bond wandering up to Lame, asking for directions, only for Lame to gesticulate at the sign — which already holds the answer. Come on Bond — one too many martinis?

"You will meet a familiar face in Vietnam," IO Interactive wrote. "Khaby Lame joins the cast of 007 First Light and is part of a globetrotting adventure as James Bond sets out to #EarnTheNumber. We worked closely with Khaby to get him scanned and design his outfit for this re-imagined origin story."

Responding to one Bond fan on social media asking why IO Interactive was highlighting this cameo ahead of time, video games industry analyst Daniel Ahmad suggested this reveal was meant to reach a different audience from traditional Bond fans, following a more traditional marketing campaign to date.

007 First Light screenshots

After rising to social media stardom over the past couple of years, Lame remains the most-followed user on TikTok. Beyond social media, Fortnite featured Lame in a 2023 battle pass, the same year he appeared as a judge on Italia's Got Talent. He also popped up with a cameo appearance in 2024's so-so Will Smith sequel Bad Boys: Ride or Die. In January of this year, Lame licensed his likeness to be reproduced by AI for $975 million.

Over the weekend, IO Interactive published a portion of the opening mission from 007 First Light after copies of the game leaked to the public days before launch. The game's early access launch begins today, May 26, with its full release coming tomorrow, May 27.

Image credit: Sam Barnes/Sportsfile for Web Summit via Getty Images.

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social

item.title: Report: Wizards of the Coast is trying to discourage workers from unionizing
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

May 26, 2026

3 Min Read

Image via Wizards of the Coast

Wizards of the Coast has reportedly been issuing letters to employees in an effort to dissuade them from voting to unionize.

Last month, a supermajority of Magic: The Gathering Arena developers signed union cards and signalled their intent to organize under the Communications Workers of America (CWA) banner.

That cohort called on Wizards of the Coast and parent company Hasbro to voluntarily recognize the union, United Wizards of the Coast—CWA, by May 1. That deadline passed without an official response to union organizers. 

As a result, employees will now decide whether to unionize by participating in a National Labour Relations Board election on June 2, 2026. With that vote around the corner, it appears Wizards of the Coast has been delivering letters to employees warning them of the apparent pitfalls of unionization.

As spotted by Kotaku, multiple Wizards of the Coast employees have shared images of that letter on social media. The correspondence describes union membership as joining forces with a "third party" and suggests that voting to unionize could result in members ending up with "less than you have now." 

Related:Subnautica 2 hits four million sales

It also states that by rejecting union representation, Wizards of the Coast will be able to work directly with employees on workplace matters "with a much greater degree of flexibility than a typically rigid union contact."

"As you consider your decision, it may be helpful to understand how union representation works. If employees chose union representation, the CWA will serve as the employees' representative in collective bargaining with the company. That process typically involves negotiationing over wages, benefits, scheduling, and other terms and conditions of employment," reads the letter.

"Outcomes are not predetermine and depend on negotiations between both parties. All the benefits and perks you currently enjoy would be on the bargaining table for both parties to negotiate, based on what's most important to them. That means you could end up with more, the same, or less than you have now. No union can guarantee what would happen in that bargaining process, which can be lengthy (465 days on average)."

The CWA has since issued a firm rebuttal to those claims on Bluesky.

"Saying 'It may be helpful to understand how union representation works' before proceeding to spread old lies about how union representation works is unfortunately both crazy and very predictable," said the organisation. "A union is not a third party representing the workers. A union is the workers themselves."

Related:Take-Two expects to earn $8B in FY27 thanks to Grand Theft Auto VI

United Wizards of the Coast—CWA organizers have explained workers are seeking to unionize in order to secure AI protections and bolster job security. The union has also called out Wizards of the Coast for issuing a widespread return to office mandate that it claims "sent a wave of anxiety and uncertainty" cascading throughout the Magic: The Gathering Arena team—all of whom have been working remotely since the beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic. 

A lack of transparency around career progression and compensation has been identified as another pain point by union organizers, who claim Wizards of the Coast has "continued to acknowledge a backlog in deserved promotions" while only promoting a handful of workers each year. 

"High-performing employees are used to proving over and over that they exceed the expectations of their role, only to end up at the back of a long line of well deserving people. This has created an environment where many employees end up working above their level for years at a time - waiting until it’s their turn to be allotted one of the scarce promotions," reads a post on the union website.

Game Developer has reached out to Wizards of the Coast for comment. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/report-wizards-of-the-coast-is-trying-to-discourage-workers-from-unionizing

item.title: It looks like a Planet Zoo 2 reveal could be just around the corner
item.content:encoded:
Following last year's sequel tease.

We already knew it was coming thanks to a brisk tease and subsequent announcement last year, but it looks like a proper Planet Zoo 2 unveiling could be imminent if an update to the series' official YouTube page is any indication.

The original Planet Zoo launched back in 2019, building upon the success of Frontier Developments' theme-park-flavoured Planet Coaster with a fresh bout of management action, this time focussed on animals rather than giant metallic vomit machines. And it was good! Bringing some welcome depth to the 'Planet' formula as park building and tycoon-ery collided with animal acquisition, breeding, care, and enrichment.

As for a potential sequel, Frontier teased it was in the works last November, cheekily sticking the number '2' at the end of a Planet Zoo six-year anniversary video. And just in case that was too subtle, it was far more explicit about it in a subsequent news post, writing, "We are delighted to reveal that we are hard at work on a sequel to Planet Zoo" and that it was looking forward to "sharing more with you next year!"

And lo, the studio has now updated its official Planet Zoo YouTube channel with an olive-hued Planet Zoo 2 logo which tells us precisely nothing beyond the fact the series is sticking to chronological naming conventions. It does, however, suggest Frontier is readying for a proper unveiling sometime soon. When? That remains a mystery for now, but seeing as we're only a week or so away from this year's Summer Games Fest, my money's on... sometime then.

Planet Zoo 2's arrival follows 2024's disappointing Planet Coaster 2, which was so barebones at launch it mostly felt like a cynical framework for DLC. However, Frontier faced a community backlash so severe after attempting to flog an undernourished DLC pack less than a month later, it retreated to focus on a roadmap of free updates that left the game in substantially better shape. Hopefully lessons have been learned for this Planet Zoo sequel, and it doesn't seem like it'll be long before we find out.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/planet-zoo-2-reveal-tease-youtube

item.title: Ermal Price Changes & New Stock Arrive In ARC Raiders
item.content:encoded:
The first round of balance changes has officially arrived for Ermal, the newest trader in ARC Raiders. After players spent the past week complaining about the steep requirements tied to his shop, Embark Studios has now significantly reduced the cost of Expedition Vault Tiers.

For those unfamiliar, Ermal is a recently added trader who offers a rotating weekly inventory, alongside upgrades that let players expand their stash space and secure items between Expeditions. However, unlike the game’s other traders, Ermal’s stock comes at a hefty price, often requiring valuable High Tier ARC parts and weapons in exchange.

Ermal Receives First Update In ARC Raiders

The biggest change in the update is easily the new pricing structure for Expedition Vault Tiers. Previously, unlocking all five slots would set players back a massive 3,000,000, with each additional slot increasing in price by 200,000.

Embark has now dramatically lowered those costs. Instead of scaling upwards, every Vault Tier now costs a flat 200,000 each. That means unlocking all five slots now totals just 1,000,000 overall, cutting the original price by a huge 2,000,000.

Alongside the pricing changes, Ermal’s store rotation has also refreshed with a brand-new selection of items available for trade. While many players still feel some prices remain on the expensive side, Embark has already stated that Ermal’s economy will continue to evolve as the studio gathers feedback and fine-tunes the balance over the coming weeks.

Here’s the new store selection:

What do you make of the changes made to Ermal? Is he still too expensive to trade with? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server, and don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28!

item.title: Embark Clarifies Live Service Update Schedule For ARC Raiders
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios has revealed how the future of live service updates will work for ARC Raiders, with players now able to expect new updates roughly every three weeks.

The news follows the studio’s earlier announcement that ARC Raiders would shift towards just two major updates per year, with the goal of making each release larger, more meaningful, and packed with content. The next major update, Frozen Trails, is currently scheduled for October, but it seems players won’t need to wait that long for fresh additions to the game.

New Updates Every Three Weeks For ARC Raiders

In a recent blog post discussing updated trading prices for Ermal, Embark quietly confirmed that ARC Raiders will receive live updates every three weeks moving forward. However, the studio also noted that these updates will vary in both size and scope, meaning not every patch will bring huge additions.

The first example of this new structure has arguably already arrived, with the introduction of Ermal alongside the Rascal grenade launcher. Since that update only landed last week, the new cadence suggests another notable update could potentially arrive in around two weeks’ time, possibly around June 9.

Naturally, that’s still speculation, but there’s certainly plenty of content waiting in the wings. Numerous leaks and datamines have already hinted at upcoming Map Conditions, weapons, and live events, all of which could serve as smaller-scale additions between the game’s larger six-month expansions.

Thankfully, fans may not have to wait much longer for more clarity. Another roadmap is expected to arrive soon, and it’s rumored to be far more detailed than the previous one, which many players felt failed to live up to expectations following the underwhelming Riven Tides update.

How do you feel about the new update schedule for ARC Raiders? Will it be enough to keep you occupied between the big updates? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server, and don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28!

For more gaming news, read about the latest Elden Ring movie leaks, as well as the popular Baki character coming to Tekken 8.

item.title: All Fortnite Community Day Free Rewards & How to Get Them
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite’s latest Community Day is on the horizon and is set to distribute a host of free rewards for players, alongside major additions to the Item Shop.

Fortnite Community Day 2026 is set for May 30, and Epic Games is going all-out with free rewards, eye-catching additions to the shop, and a host of in-game content to enjoy.

If you want to make sure you don’t miss a single reward, Insider Gaming has all the details you need.

Fortnite is distributing a bunch of free rewards on Community Day, with four brand-new additions and the return of some Twitch Drop items.

All players are entitled to three free login rewards during the celebrations, while Battle Pass owners will receive a Workplace style for the Foundation Skin.

  • More Insider Gaming: When is Fortnite Community Day 2026?

Meanwhile, three Twitch Drop rewards have been revealed for the event, all of which were first available during the Shock ‘N Awesome season launch in November 2025, giving those who missed out another chance to claim the items.

The table below details all the free rewards available in Fortnite’s Community Day 2026, and the method to obtain them. Additional rewards will be added as they are revealed.

Alongside the free rewards, new and returning Shop Items will be available to purchase, including the launch of Typical Gamers’ Icon Series skin.

Typical Gamer becomes the latest in a long line of content creators and celebrities to receive a Fortnite collab, following on from a wide array of faces, including the likes of IShowSpeed, Sabrina Carpenter, and characters from K-Pop Demon Hunters.

  • More Insider Gaming: When is The Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Live Event?

Alongside that debut, the return of the Indigo Kuno skin to the Item Shop is coming after a long five years of waiting, opening the doors for more players to receive the skin, which was previously only available for 6,000 players worldwide.

Additional Shop reveals are expected in the countdown to the launch of Community Day.

What are you most excited about from the Fortnite Community Day? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 players are being tormented by 'drivatar' bowieknife99, but the real racer behind the bot remains a mystery
item.content:encoded:
"There's a man in the woods."

Forza Horizon 6 is a great time in an extremely detailed replica of Japan, but a single 'drivatar' is causing mayhem across the racing game, with the community now organising to end their reign of terror and learn more about their origins.

Last week, players quickly noticed a recurring foe who was performing a little too well and even managed to surprise with unpredictable moves. In the Forza Horizon games, AI bots based on your friends and other community members populate offline and co-op races; 'drivatars' is how the games refer to them. This time around, the 'drivatar' known as bowieknife99 has become a boogeyman of sorts.

Unless we're being misled, bowieknife99 is based on a real player too, which has prompted investigations into who this person is, but their Xbox profile is set to private. As it stands, the individual behind the myth remains a mystery. Meanwhile, the bot is exhibiting unexpected behaviour, ramming into players and even driving off the road to hit their targets. It must be noted 'drivatars' are typically logical in their decision-making and stick to the courses even if they're hard to beat at times.

"Thought I had this drag race in the bag, JUST FOR THE CAMERA TO TURN AROUND AND SHOW ME LOSING TO A TRICYCLE," a player lamented as they shared a clip of the one and only bowieknife99 zooming into the horizon with a tiny, ridiculous ride putting all the beefier cars to shame.

Of course, some players are trying to take matters into their own hands, specifically targeting the 'drivatar' where possible, but results are mixed so far. As the legend grows, we demand justice. In my own experience, bowieknife99 hasn't been especially weird, but they're a rival to beat in almost every offline or co-op race for sure. I'm personally praying developer Playground Games never fixes whatever is wrong with that bot. After all, it's nice to have a common enemy.

Mentioned in this article

item.title: One Of 2025's Best Games Is Now Free For Switch Online Members
item.content:encoded:
Play it until 1st June

Nintendo has announced that BALL x PIT has become the latest title to receive a free trial for Switch Online subscribers.

Available for members in Europe from now until 1st June 2026, you can download the Switch version for absolutely nothing, while the Upgrade Pack to enhance the game for Switch 2 is also free. Boom!

It's the full game too, of course, so if you have literally no plans over the next handful of days, you could probably make some decent progress before the trial period ends.

So if you've seen the odd trailer here and there, or maybe you caught our own 9/10 review but have yet to take the plunge, this could be your moment, friend.

Reviewer Roland Ingram said:

"Ball x Pit is a tremendous brew of so many ideas it ought to collapse under the confusion. However, it operates in such perfect balance that it appeals both to the one-more-go instinct and to more cerebral planning and creativity. Kenny Sun Studios set itself a heck of a challenge but, fortunately, hasn’t dropped the ball."

"Hasn't dropped the ball", that's a good one there from Roland.

Anyway, give it a go – we think you'll enjoy it.

Will you be taking Ball x Pit for a spin? Or have you already had your fill? Let us know with a comment in the usual place.

[source x.com]

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.
